name: Sidney De Paris
abstract: Sidney De Paris (May 30, 1905 – September 13, 1967) was an American jazz trumpeter. His brother was Wilbur de Paris.

concerts.label: Jazz Concert in Memory of Sidney Bechet

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: I met another musician whom I had known since childhood at the Savoy. He was Sidney de Paris whom I had known in Crawfordsville, Indiana when I was 6 years old.

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Waters
about_quotes.evidence: I'm working with a great band with Charlie Johnson, such guys as Jimmy Harrison, all that kind of stuff - Sidney de Paris... Dickie Wells and Sidney de Paris

about_quotes.speaker: Mario Bauza
about_quotes.evidence: the guys that hang around there were all like guys like ... Wilbur de Paris, Sidney de Paris, all the big musicians in those days

about_quotes.speaker: George Wein
about_quotes.evidence: He had Wilber and Sidney de Paris and Big Sid Catlett on the drums... That band cost me a thousand dollars a week.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Wein — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: William Randolph "Cozy" Cole
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Yeah, right, Benny Carter was in that band. Wilbur de Paris, Sidney de Paris, Cy St. Claire, bass.

about_quotes.speaker: Danny Barker
about_quotes.evidence: I worked with them for about three months, then I quit. Wilbur De Paris and Sidney De Paris...
